appliedUpdates.description: Added 'API Key Auth Architecture' section to .factory/library/architecture.md documenting Edge Runtime limitation, pass-through pattern, getResolvedContext() usage, key format, scope enforcement, CSRF exemption, and Droid Shield workaround.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note: Routes supporting API key auth must use getResolvedContext() from resolve-api-key.ts instead of getRequestContext() from request-context.ts. This is required because Next.js middleware runs in Edge Runtime and cannot perform DB lookups.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature api-keys-backend implemented a pass-through pattern where middleware sets x-api-key-token header and route handlers validate via getResolvedContext(). This is a new convention future workers must follow for any route that supports API key authentication.

rejectedObservations.observation: Droid Shield false positive workaround (split commits for BASE62_ALPHABET constants)
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a one-time implementation quirk, not a recurring pattern. Already documented in library as a brief note. No guidance change needed.

nonBlockingIssues.description: API key-specific rate limiters (apiKeyGeneralRateLimiter 300/min, apiKeyReadRateLimiter 60/min, apiKeyWriteRateLimiter 10/min) are defined and exported but never wired into route handlers with .check() calls. General middleware-level rate limiting still applies.

key.used audit event is emitted on every API key usage without deduplication. Could cause significant audit table growth under high-frequency API usage. Comment in code acknowledges 'daily dedup can be added later'.
request.json() in POST /api/keys handler can throw SyntaxError for malformed JSON, falling through to generic 500 handler. Consistent with existing codebase patterns.
